svg.title: Nested ladder of nucleotide substitution models
svg.desc: A six-step ladder of nucleotide substitution models, from Jukes-Cantor at the bottom to GTR plus gamma plus invariant sites at the top. Each step adds parameters. Each panel shows the 4-by-4 rate matrix as a small grid with cells coloured by relative magnitude, and lists the free parameter count and the empirical use case. A footer table summarises the AIC ranking on a typical phylogenetic dataset.
svg.text: NUCLEOTIDE SUBSTITUTION MODELS — A NESTED LADDER
Each step adds free parameters. The AIC / BIC penalty trades fit against complexity; ModelFinder auto-picks the best for your data.

g.text: All six off-diagonals free under detailed balance.
Each model to the right contains every model to its left as a special case (equal-parameter restriction).
Per-site rate ~ Gamma(α, α); usually discretised into 4 categories. Small α = strong heterogeneity.
A fraction p_inv of positions are frozen by selection (rate = 0). Captures conserved active sites and rRNA stems.
Vertebrate cytochrome-b, 50 species, 1140 sites. AIC, lower is better.
representative values; real ΔAIC depends on the data.

svg.text: Detailed-balance constraint reduces 12 directed off-diagonals to 6 symmetric rates; equilibrium frequencies sum to 1 (3 free parameters).
